debugging-imports-and-state.label: Debugging Imports and State
debugging-imports-and-state.summary: Fix common multi-file OOP bugs by following imports, object state, and repeated method calls.

try_debugging_imports_and_state_sketch0.title: Try it yourself
try_debugging_imports_and_state_sketch0.prompt: Fix the import so `main.py` can create a `Customer` from `models/customer.py`.

try_debugging_imports_and_state_sketch1.title: Try it yourself
try_debugging_imports_and_state_sketch1.prompt: Move the task list onto each instance so two projects do not share the same list.

ci-debug-order-practice.title: Fix object state update order
ci-debug-order-practice.prompt: Debug the cart so adding an item updates the total before the summary is printed.
ci-debug-order-practice.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

help.concept: In a multi-file project, each file should own one clear responsibility and imports should connect those responsibilities.
help.hint_1: Run from the entry file and follow the first failing import or assertion.
help.hint_2: Keep the full project state working, not just the one line you changed.

ci-debug-order-practice.starterCode:
from models.cart import Cart

cart = Cart()
cart.add_item("Pen", 2)
cart.add_item("Bag", 5)
print(cart.summary())

sc-debugging-imports-and-state-design.title: Check the design choice
sc-debugging-imports-and-state-design.prompt: Which choice best matches the lesson goal for debugging imports and state?
sc-debugging-imports-and-state-design.hint: Choose the option that keeps object responsibilities clear.

help.concept: Fix common multi-file OOP bugs by following imports, object state, and repeated method calls.
help.hint_1: Look for the answer that reduces duplication without hiding behavior.
help.hint_2: A good OOP answer usually keeps behavior near the object that owns the data.

options.a: Put every class in main.py so imports are never needed.
options.b: Give each class or service a focused responsibility and import it where needed.
options.c: Avoid methods so every object exposes all of its data directly.
options.d: Copy the same logic into each subclass to avoid shared code.

try-debugging-imports-and-state-sketch0.prompt: Fix the import so `main.py` can create a `Customer` from `models/customer.py`.
try-debugging-imports-and-state-sketch0.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

help.concept: In a multi-file project, each file should own one clear responsibility and imports should connect those responsibilities.
help.hint_1: Run from the entry file and follow the first failing import or assertion.
help.hint_2: Keep the full project state working, not just the one line you changed.

try-debugging-imports-and-state-sketch0.starterCode:
from customer import Customer

customer = Customer("Lina")
print(customer.name)

try-debugging-imports-and-state-sketch1.title: Fix shared list state
try-debugging-imports-and-state-sketch1.prompt: Move the task list onto each instance so two projects do not share the same list.
try-debugging-imports-and-state-sketch1.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

help.concept: In a multi-file project, each file should own one clear responsibility and imports should connect those responsibilities.
help.hint_1: Run from the entry file and follow the first failing import or assertion.
help.hint_2: Keep the full project state working, not just the one line you changed.

try-debugging-imports-and-state-sketch1.starterCode:
from models.project import Project

a = Project("A")
b = Project("B")
a.add_task("plan")
print(a.count_tasks())
print(b.count_tasks())

concept.title: Debugging Imports and State: the idea
concept.bodyMarkdown: **Debugging Imports and State** is not just syntax. In this course it is practiced inside small projects where one file owns one clear part of the program. Fix common multi-file OOP bugs by following imports, object state, and repeated method calls.

multifile-shape.title: Debugging Imports and State: the file shape
multifile-shape.bodyMarkdown: The file layout matters. Models hold object state and behavior, services coordinate groups of objects, tests verify behavior, and `main.py` stays thin enough to read.

graded-habit.title: Debugging Imports and State: what the grader checks
graded-habit.bodyMarkdown: The grader checks behavior from the running project. A strong answer keeps imports working, keeps all files present, and proves the object behavior through output or assertions.
